page: Microsoft Azure D8plds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine instance powered by an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor. Operating at a stable frequency of 3.0 GHz, this instance features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, utilizing a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration that avoids hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It is equipped with 307 GB of local SSD storage and supports Premium Storage.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the average tier for most multi-core and memory operations. The system achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 14501.82 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 6013.0. Memory bandwidth is notable, reaching 565.84 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, though it drops to 84.32 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1057.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 6130.18.

From a resource density perspective, the D8plds_v5 offers a cost-effective profile for workloads that require dedicated physical cores but do not demand high memory allocations. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a tradeoff, making it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or large-scale LLM inference, where text generation speeds for a 7B model reach only 7.58 tokens/sec. However, it demonstrates solid efficiency for static web serving, achieving 9.92 GB/sec throughput, and Redis SET operations at 10.26 million operations per second.

This instance is best suited for scale-out cloud workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight database operations. The inclusion of 307 GB of local storage provides a fast scratch space for temporary files, making it a viable option for CI/CD pipelines and application servers that benefit from dedicated ARM architecture without the overhead of hyperthreading.
description: Microsoft Azure D8plds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and includes 307 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage support. Benchmarks show average multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6013.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 14501.82, alongside a high cached read memory bandwidth of 565.84 GB/sec. However, single-core metrics and memory-heavy tasks show weaker results, such as a PassMark CPU score of 6130.18. This resource profile offers a cost-effective option for compute-focused workloads with low memory requirements. It is highly suitable for static web serving, software compilation, and lightweight database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8plds_v5 is an ARM-based instance with 8 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 16 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, offering balanced multi-core performance for web serving and compilation.

tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores and local SSD storage optimized for low-memory compute workloads.
